asset_name: BPC-157 Pharmacokinetics, Distribution, Metabolism and Excretion

primary_source.title: Pharmacokinetics, distribution, metabolism, and excretion of body-protective compound 157, a potential drug for treating various wounds, in rats and dogs

additional_findings: Prototype BPC-157 was not detectable four hours after administration in the reported rat and dog experiments.
The reported elimination half-life of prototype BPC-157 was less than 30 minutes across the studied animal conditions.
Urine and bile were reported as major excretory pathways in radiolabel studies.
BPC-157 was rapidly metabolized into smaller peptide fragments and amino acids.
critical_translation_limit: This is animal pharmacokinetic evidence. The authors' proposed clinical-dose assumption and body-surface-area conversions do not constitute a validated human dosing protocol.
funding: National Natural Science Foundation of China
Key Research and Development Program of Shaanxi Province
conflict_of_interest: Authors declared no commercial or financial relationships that could be construed as a potential conflict of interest.
limitations: Species differences make direct human dose translation invalid without human clinical pharmacology.
The study itself notes limited tissue metabolite analysis and a small sample size for some analyses.
